reviews.review_msg: Gail is super knowledgeable and has had a really impressive career. But unfortunately, this class was quite painful. Although the lecture videos were engaging, there are so many side assignments/discussions to complete weekly. There was no midterm, but the final exam was 120 short answer questions to complete in 3 hours. Literally impossible!!!!!!!
Such an interesting course and a prof which makes it so easy to understand and follow along. Clear instructions, assignments to be completed weekly but it's manageable. Would recommend this prof!!!

Exams were entirely written, 23-37 short answer questions, absolutely way too many written questions for the time limit. TA took 1 week to respond to emails and sometimes did not respond at all, even when asking questions in the "tutor marker office" discussion forum. No help was given by the TA or prof at all. Completely self taught class. Avoid.

Everything revolves around the textbook. Weekly readings are very long and time consuming. The content is challenging but interesting; the midterm and final are completely written short answer questions (x22 of them); the midterm was rough. Nevertheless, I recommend this as an elective purely because its online.

Class text was written by the prof, exams/assignments involve memorizing the textbook. There are no lectures, just this boring, hard to read, biology-heavy, long and drawn out textbook that reads like a literature review in an academic journal article. The text is awful and I feel that we are only told to buy it so prof can make money from sales

Hands down the best professor I have ever encountered at Simon Fraser, I think this professor is one of the reasons the Criminology department stands out at SFU across the country. High expectations, as well as a hard marker, but cares about the success of her students. I have never encountered an unfair exam. I think SFU is very lucky to have Gail

The final for this class is absolutely ridiculous. Questions based on personal opinions rather than material covered. Was doing great in the class up until the end. Beware that no amount of studying can prepare you for the irrelevant questions that are asked on the final exam - which is worth 40%

I have taken a few of her courses online and in class and she is a great prof, true expert in her field, clear expectations in courses.  Lecture material in class important for exams, textbook important for online course exams.  You learn alot.

I took this class online and it was all based on the textbook. The exams were tough, but if you study hard it shouldn't be too hard. The TA graded the exams hard. You had to take all your notes from the textbook (no modules- just a general outline of the chapter). You need to work hard to achieve a good grade.

Distance Ed, this class is not easy. Final did not test your knowledge but had some questions that were irrelevant to the course.. overall if you intersted in biology you might like it otherwise for extra credits id recommend another course. Tests were annoying, hard and no entirely fair, participation easy marks tho. TA Jodie was good.
Not great

I often wonder if teachers actually read the surveys they make us take or if they just do them to make us feel heard. I literally asked for the 3hr final exam on a weekday to be changed to a 3-day take home (during a weekend)... AND SHE DID IT! I think teachers had some options because of COVID but I didn't think she would make it open book & 3day!
she is ok
Very harsh marking rubric. Lectures are messy and material is dense. A lot of reading. Avoid.

Jodie is a great prof! Her lectures are sometimes boring as she reads off the slides but its smart to take notes on top of the notes to help you get a better understanding of the material. This class was pretty interesting, don't let the science part scare you... its all memorization. If Jodie herself marks then you're good to go! Great prof!

Jodie is one of my favourite profs at SFU. I've taken two of her classes and would definitely take another in the future. She's very knowledgeable and quite funny. Very pleasant to be around. I wish her the best on her future endeavours!

I took this class online. Both her exams are all short written questions. Make sure you stay on top of the readings if you want to do well. Good thing is you kinda bs on the exams if you somewhat know the general ideas.

very helpful exams not so easy. The midterm was brutal even though she gave us study questions. She made the final easier based on everyones average on the midterm. Final much easier. Overall very nice helpful and wants her students to do well. I would take her again.
Interesting course, Jodie was fair and helpful, exams were certainly not easy, but not difficult either. Involves a great deal of memorization.
Jodie is a great teacher. She is truly kind and cares about how you do in the class. I am so happy to be taking a second course with her this semester.

The class is 100% textbook based and additional journal articles, there are no lectures for her to give online so really she's only marking exams (with the TM). And the TM was the one to email questions to as well. Seems nice, if you find the content interesting you will do well. Mainly midterm and final though.
Suzanna is awesome, she really knows her stuff and communicates very clearly and effectively. Would totally recommend!

Stay clear of this class. The midterm and final are worth a huge chunk of your grades and they are really hard. You have to study very specific details and a lot of important concepts that we had to study was not even on the exams. This was a total disappointment
The exam's for this course are HARSH. Very specific information and they are 100 point exams. Get ready to fail.

She is kinda hot for someone with kids. Assignments are easy and exams are decent. Only beef is that during exams, sometimes she talks loudly to students, cracks jokes and basically acts unprofessional.
prof???? she wishes...thinks shes a prof but doesnt have a hope. full of herself.
This prof wants you to work hard, but is willing to help you if you are willing to help yourself.

Easy class, easy marker.  At times she did not really know what she was talking about, again only knowledgeable in her own research area. Easy exams, short answer straight from notes.  not a challenging course!!
